summary: Finished the match for the sword item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itsword.s with src/melee/it/items/itsword.c in the build, marking itsword.c as Matching, and cleaning up C source patterns that prevented matching. The patch removed the 1467-line assembly file, adjusted build object selection, changed the sword item variable x0 type from UNK_T to int, replaced local float/double constants with direct literals, corrected several accesses from the capsule/star union aliases to sword fields, and consolidated repeated inline helper bodies used by sword state transitions.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/it/items/itsword.s, removing the assembly fallback for the sword item module.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itsword.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itsword.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itsword.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/itsword.s.o.
In itCommonItems.h, changed struct itSword_ItemVars field x0 at ip+DD4 from UNK_T to int.

Removed local static constants such as it_804DC8D0, it_804DC8D4, it_804DC8D8, it_804DC8DC, it_804DC8E0, it_804DC8E8, it_804DC8F0, it_804DC8F4, and it_804DC8F8, replacing their uses with direct literals like 0.0f, -1.0f, 0.3f, 1.0f, 1.5f, 2.0f, 10.03f, and division by 4.
Changed spawn.facing_dir assignment from -1 to -1.0f, matching the float nature of the field and avoiding integer literal codegen differences.
Corrected sword item variable accesses that previously went through other union views, including capsule.x0/capsule.x4 and star xvel/yvel aliases, to the sword-specific fields.

Consolidated duplicated inline setup helpers: it_802855F8, it_80285B00, it_80285C78, and it_80285DF0 now reuse inlineA1, inlineA2, and inlineA3 where prior code had near-duplicate inlineB/inlineE/inlineF/inlineG helpers.
Added/repositioned inlineB0, inlineC1, and inlineD1 helper bodies near the shared helper cluster, then removed older duplicate helper implementations later in the file.
Replaced numeric animation/drop update flag value 6 with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE | ITEM_DROP_UPDATE in calls to Item_80268E5C for sword item state changes.
Simplified some JObj child access from a two-step GET_JOBJ temporary to HSD_JObjGetChild(GET_JOBJ(gobj)) in affected sword state functions.
smart_moves: Converted the build only after the C file matched, evidenced by deleting the asm file and switching both configure.py and obj_files.mk together.
Removed small local constant symbols and used typed float literals directly; this is a common matching lever when literal pool placement or symbolized constants create codegen differences.
Used explicit float suffixes for values such as -1.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f, 0.3f, and 10.03f to control floating-point codegen.
Reduced many duplicate inline helpers to shared inlineA1/inlineA2/inlineA3 calls, which likely helped the compiler emit the same repeated setup sequence across multiple action/state functions.
Fixed union member naming to the actual sword item variable view, improving both readability and type/codegen accuracy without changing the underlying offsets.
Changed an unknown sword variable field from UNK_T to int, which documents the field width/type and likely avoids casts or ambiguous stores.
decomp_lessons: For item modules with large repeated state setup blocks, look for identical inline helper bodies that can be factored into one helper and reused to match repeated compiler output.

When a unioned item-var struct is accessed through unrelated aliases such as capsule or star, verify whether the current item has a named struct view and switch to that view for maintainability and type clarity.
Typed literals matter: integer-looking assignments to float fields, or double-vs-float constants, can prevent matching even when the source looks semantically equivalent.
Replacing magic numeric flag values with existing flag macros can preserve codegen while making intent clearer, as shown by using ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE | ITEM_DROP_UPDATE for value 6.
A completed match should update all three places consistently: remove the asm file, mark the C object Matching in configure.py, and switch obj_files.mk to the C object.
Header type refinements can be part of a match fix; changing UNK_T to int at a specific item-var offset may unblock cleaner C source and better compiler output.
naming_conventions: Sword item variables are stored under ip->xDD4_itemVar.sword and retain offset-style field names such as x0, x4, x10, x28, x40, x4C, and x54.
The project continues to use address-derived function names for unmatched/unnamed item routines, e.g. it_80284E30, it_80285084, it_802852B8, it_80285920, and it_80285B00.
Local inline helpers in this file use temporary alphabetical names such as inlineA0, inlineA1, inlineA2, inlineA3, inlineA4, inlineB0, inlineC1, and inlineD1.
Build configuration uses Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itsword.c") once the source replaces its asm fallback.
Item update flag names ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and ITEM_DROP_UPDATE are preferred over a raw combined numeric value when available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the assembly fallback after the C source matched, removing asm/melee/it/items/itsword.s from the build.
Switched obj_files.mk from an asm object path to the C object path for itsword.
Marked the source as Matching in configure.py to reflect that the C implementation now reproduces target code.
Replaced file-local constant symbols with immediate typed literals, likely to alter literal placement and floating-point instruction selection.
Factored repeated setup sequences into reused static inline helpers to influence inlining and reduce mismatched duplicate code shapes.
Used explicit direct division by 4 in inlineC1 instead of the previous multiplication by 0.25 constant, a small expression-shape change that can affect generated floating-point code.
Simplified temporaries around HSD_JObjGetChild(GET_JOBJ(gobj)) in some functions, which may have helped register allocation or stack layout.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in this PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find other item modules that still access xDD4_itemVar through unrelated union aliases like capsule or star.
Search for remaining item C files with local static float constants that could be replaced by typed literals for matching.
Search for repeated inline helper families similar to inlineA1/inlineA2/inlineA3 in other item modules.
Check current itsword.c for whether address-derived names like it_80284E30 and inlineA1 have since been given semantic names.
Find PRs that changed obj_files.mk from asm item objects to src item objects to compare matching tactics.
